instruction: Deploy a WordPress application on my OneKE cluster
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Deploy WordPress application using the Bitnami Helm chart on OneKE"
  target_cluster: "default"
  estimated_duration: "5-10 minutes"
  risk_level: "low"
  tags: ["oneke", "wordpress", "deployment"]

steps:
  - id: "step_01"
    action: "oneke.namespace.create"
    description: "Create the wordpress namespace for isolating the deployment"
    params:
      name: "wordpress"
      labels:
        app: "wordpress"
        managed-by: "one-ai"
    on_failure: "abort"

  - id: "step_02"
    action: "oneke.app.deploy"
    description: "Deploy WordPress using the Bitnami Helm chart with default configuration"
    params:
      chart: "bitnami/wordpress"
      namespace: "wordpress"
      release_name: "wordpress"
      repo_url: "https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami"
      create_namespace: false
      wait: true
      values:
        wordpressUsername: "admin"
        service:
          type: "LoadBalancer"
        persistence:
          enabled: true
          size: "10Gi"
    depends_on: ["step_01"]
    on_failure: "rollback"
    timeout_seconds: 300

  - id: "step_03"
    action: "oneke.app.wait_ready"
    description: "Wait for all WordPress pods to reach ready state"
    params:
      namespace: "wordpress"
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=wordpress"
      timeout_seconds: 300
    depends_on: ["step_02"]
    on_failure: "rollback"

  - id: "step_04"
    action: "oneke.service.get_endpoint"
    description: "Retrieve the external endpoint URL for accessing WordPress"
    params:
      namespace: "wordpress"
      service_name: "wordpress"
    depends_on: ["step_03"]

validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: "cluster_reachable"
      target: "default"
    - type: "namespace_available"
      namespace: "wordpress"
    - type: "helm_repo_available"
      target: "https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami"
  post_checks:
    - type: "pods_running"
      namespace: "wordpress"
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=wordpress"
      expected_count: 1
    - type: "service_available"
      namespace: "wordpress"
      service_name: "wordpress"

rollback:
  steps:
    - id: "step_01"
      action: "oneke.app.uninstall"
      description: "Remove the WordPress Helm release"
      params:
        release_name: "wordpress"
        namespace: "wordpress"
    - id: "step_02"
      action: "oneke.namespace.delete"
      description: "Clean up the wordpress namespace"
      params:
        name: "wordpress"
      depends_on: ["step_01"]

instruction: Remove the Redis deployment from the staging namespace
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Uninstall Redis Helm release from the staging namespace"
  target_cluster: "default"
  estimated_duration: "1-2 minutes"
  risk_level: "medium"
  tags: ["oneke", "redis", "uninstall"]

steps:
  - id: "step_01"
    action: "oneke.app.get_status"
    description: "Verify that the Redis release exists in the staging namespace before removal"
    params:
      release_name: "redis"
      namespace: "staging"
    on_failure: "abort"

  - id: "step_02"
    action: "oneke.app.uninstall"
    description: "Uninstall the Redis Helm release from staging"
    params:
      release_name: "redis"
      namespace: "staging"
      keep_history: false
    depends_on: ["step_01"]
    on_failure: "abort"

validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: "cluster_reachable"
      target: "default"
    - type: "namespace_exists"
      namespace: "staging"
  post_checks:
    - type: "pods_running"
      namespace: "staging"
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=redis"
      expected_count: 0

rollback:
  steps: []

instruction: Scale the worker nodes in my OneKE cluster to 5
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Scale the OneKE cluster worker pool to 5 nodes"
  target_cluster: "default"
  estimated_duration: "5-15 minutes"
  risk_level: "medium"
  tags: ["oneke", "cluster", "scaling"]

steps:
  - id: "step_01"
    action: "oneke.cluster.get_info"
    description: "Get current cluster information to verify state before scaling"
    params:
      cluster_name: "default"
    on_failure: "abort"

  - id: "step_02"
    action: "oneke.cluster.list_nodes"
    description: "List current worker nodes to understand the current scale"
    params:
      cluster_name: "default"
      role: "worker"
    depends_on: ["step_01"]

  - id: "step_03"
    action: "oneke.cluster.scale_nodes"
    description: "Scale the worker node pool to 5 nodes"
    params:
      cluster_name: "default"
      worker_count: 5
    depends_on: ["step_02"]
    on_failure: "rollback"
    timeout_seconds: 600

validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: "cluster_reachable"
      target: "default"
  post_checks:
    - type: "pods_running"
      namespace: "kube-system"
      label_selector: "node-role.kubernetes.io/worker"
      expected_count: 5

rollback:
  steps: []

instruction: I want to deploy a machine learning model using TensorFlow Serving on my OpenNebula cluster without Kubernetes
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Unable to fulfill request - TensorFlow Serving deployment without Kubernetes is not directly supported"
  risk_level: "low"
  tags: ["error", "unsupported"]

steps: []

error:
  is_error: true
  reason: "Deploying TensorFlow Serving directly on OpenNebula VMs without Kubernetes is not currently supported by this tool. The tool currently supports OneKE (OpenNebula's Kubernetes Engine) deployments via Helm charts. Deploying TensorFlow Serving as a bare VM application would require manual VM provisioning, Docker installation, model file management, and service configuration that goes beyond the current automation capabilities."
  suggestion: "Consider one of these alternatives: (1) Use OneKE to deploy TensorFlow Serving as a Kubernetes deployment via Helm chart — ask me to 'Deploy TensorFlow Serving on my OneKE cluster'. (2) If you need bare-metal performance, you could manually create a VM template with TensorFlow Serving pre-installed and use OpenNebula's VM management to deploy it."

validation:
  pre_checks: []
  post_checks: []

rollback:
  steps: []

instruction: Create a new namespace called 'production' and deploy a Nginx ingress controller in it
output:
version: "1.0"
metadata:
  description: "Create production namespace and deploy Nginx Ingress Controller via Helm"
  target_cluster: "default"
  estimated_duration: "3-5 minutes"
  risk_level: "low"
  tags: ["oneke", "nginx", "ingress", "production"]

steps:
  - id: "step_01"
    action: "oneke.namespace.create"
    description: "Create the production namespace with appropriate labels"
    params:
      name: "production"
      labels:
        environment: "production"
        managed-by: "one-ai"
    on_failure: "abort"

  - id: "step_02"
    action: "oneke.app.deploy"
    description: "Deploy the Nginx Ingress Controller Helm chart into the production namespace"
    params:
      chart: "ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx"
      namespace: "production"
      release_name: "nginx-ingress"
      repo_url: "https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx"
      create_namespace: false
      wait: true
      values:
        controller:
          replicaCount: 2
          service:
            type: "LoadBalancer"
    depends_on: ["step_01"]
    on_failure: "rollback"
    timeout_seconds: 300

  - id: "step_03"
    action: "oneke.app.wait_ready"
    description: "Wait for Nginx Ingress Controller pods to be ready"
    params:
      namespace: "production"
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=ingress-nginx"
      timeout_seconds: 180
      expected_replicas: 2
    depends_on: ["step_02"]

  - id: "step_04"
    action: "oneke.service.get_endpoint"
    description: "Get the external IP assigned to the ingress controller LoadBalancer"
    params:
      namespace: "production"
      service_name: "nginx-ingress-ingress-nginx-controller"
    depends_on: ["step_03"]

validation:
  pre_checks:
    - type: "cluster_reachable"
      target: "default"
    - type: "namespace_available"
      namespace: "production"
  post_checks:
    - type: "pods_running"
      namespace: "production"
      label_selector: "app.kubernetes.io/name=ingress-nginx"
      expected_count: 2
    - type: "service_available"
      namespace: "production"
      service_name: "nginx-ingress-ingress-nginx-controller"

rollback:
  steps:
    - id: "step_01"
      action: "oneke.app.uninstall"
      description: "Remove the Nginx Ingress Controller release"
      params:
        release_name: "nginx-ingress"
        namespace: "production"
    - id: "step_02"
      action: "oneke.namespace.delete"
      description: "Remove the production namespace"
      params:
        name: "production"
      depends_on: ["step_01"]
